key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: Two-year average FY2023–FY2024 free cash flow per share $0.16 (FY2025 trough cash burn $0.42/sh excluded from base normalization) per AEHR/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20250728_rpt20250530_acc0001654954_25_008553.htm
Partial SiC / AI burn-in recovery from FY2025 trough

open_questions: Confirm mid-cycle **$0.16/sh** vs spot trough **($0.42)/sh** before stance promotion
April 2026 **$60M** ATM: track issuance vs SiC order recovery
Insider sales (CFO July 2026) vs director purchases at low prices — context only
Live price confirmation flag still open in `valuation.json`
thesis: Aehr Test Systems (NASDAQ: AEHR) designs, manufactures, and sells wafer-level burn-in and test systems, led by the FOX-XP multi-wafer test platform and WaferPak contactors. **[Fact]** FY2025 consolidated revenue was **$59.0 million**, down from **$66.2 million** in FY2024 and below the **$65.0 million** FY2023 level that followed the SiC capacity build-out. **[Fact]** FY2025 net loss was **$3.9 million** after net income peaked at **$33.2 million** in FY2024. **[Fact]** The company reported **$36.9 million** in cash and no term debt as of February 27, 2026, with roughly **31.5 million** diluted shares outstanding. **[Inference]** Owner cash is cyclically depressed after a major customer paused orders; the equity trades as a SiC and AI burn-in recovery option at a price far above normalized component value.
why_market_wrong: The market may be pricing **SiC wafer-level burn-in and AI accelerator test recovery** while underweighting **FY2025 operating cash outflow of $7.4 million**, **customer concentration risk** disclosed in the 10-K, and **dilution overhang** from the April 2026 **$60 million** at-the-market equity program. **[Inference]** Nine-month FY2026 net income of **$8.5 million** shows partial recovery, but Q3 revenue of **$10.3 million** was down year over year; normalized mid-cycle owner cash near **$0.16 per share** supports a partial balance-sheet floor, not a high-teens annual return at **$77.36**.
